message:
fix(node): harden cancellation GC, fork detection state tracking, and retroactive hardening level computation

Cancellation protocol (cancellation_protocol.rs):
- Replace push_bounded (which blindly drops the oldest record regardless
  of state) with a two-phase GC: first retain only non-Finalized records,
  then if still at capacity drain the oldest active records as a last-resort
  OOM defense. This prevents dropping in-flight cancellation records that
  are still being coordinated while preferring to evict completed ones.

Fork detection (fork_detection.rs):
- Update self.last_known to the incoming StateVector before returning
  RfdGapDetected, so subsequent feed() calls detect gaps relative to the
  most recent observation rather than the stale pre-gap state. Without
  this, a detector that saw epoch 1 then 3 would continue reporting
  gap-from-1 on every subsequent call instead of advancing.

Retroactive hardening (retroactive_hardening.rs):
- Account for an object's creation_level when computing required
  protections: use effective_from = max(from_level, creation_level) so
  objects natively created at a higher hardening level don't receive
  redundant lower-level artifacts. Applies to both harden() (level
  escalation) and ensure_protections() (single-level enforcement).
- Add test_harden_object_already_at_target_creation_level verifying that
  an Enhanced-creation object produces no artifacts when escalated from
  Baseline to Standard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
